summary: High‑energy Boston show with stellar China Cat > Rider and tight Touch
blurb: The second set shines with a fiery China Cat Sunflower into I Know You Rider, backed by swirling drums and space. First‑set highlights include Touch of Grey, Row Jimmy and an inspired So Many Roads. Overall the band is in peak ’93 form, delivering tight, bass‑driven grooves.
review:
September 28, 1993 at Boston Garden finds the Dead firing on all cylinders. The first set opens with a bright "Touch of Grey" that rolls straight into a punchy "Greatest Story Ever Told," then builds momentum through "Row Jimmy" and lands on a soulful rendition of "So Many Roads" where Jerry’s voice shines—aside from a minor slip on the final verse, his guitar tone remains solid throughout. The second set is the show‑stopper: a blazing "China Cat Sunflower" segues into a soaring "I Know You Rider," followed by a deep groove in "Samson & Delilah" and a marathon "Playing In The Band" that erupts into the classic drum‑and‑space excursion, delivering the swirling, circular sound that defines the early ’90s Dead. The set closes with an uplifting "Throwing Stones" into a feel‑good "Good Lovin'" and a rousing encore of "U.S. Blues." 

For listeners wanting the clearest picture, the community‑recommended mixed audience/soundboard master (gd93-09-28.aud-sbd.miller.12850.shnf) captures the concert’s energy while preserving the hall ambience that many fans love. The pure soundboard version (gd1993-09-28.sbd.miller.79058) offers an "incredible" texture for those who prefer a pristine board mix, but the audience‑enhanced blend remains the most balanced listening experience.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Three recordings are clearly positive, praising the high‑energy second set, standout China Cat > Rider, Touch of Grey, Row Jimmy and So Many Roads, while noting excellent bass texture and swirling drums. Two mixed reviews do not criticize performance, only recording quirks, so overall sentiment is strongly positive without consistent negatives.

best_recording.reason: Balanced audience‑plus‑soundboard mix that captures the concert’s energy and hall ambience while avoiding major dropouts.

key_highlights: High‑energy second set with powerful drum/space segment
Stellar China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider transition
Tight, bass‑driven grooves on Touch of Grey, Row Jimmy and So Many Roads
Clear, balanced audio from the recommended audience/SBD blend

band_performance.Jerry: Great vibe and strong vocal delivery with solid guitar tone; a slight slip on the final verse of So Many Roads.
band_performance.Phil: Bass praised as "GOD" and a key element of the show’s texture, driving the tight grooves.
band_performance.Mickey: Drums (with Billy) deliver exemplary swirling, circular sound that moves the body, especially during the drum/space section.
band_performance.Billy: Together with Mickey provides a powerful rhythmic foundation and space exploration that fuels the second‑set energy.
